definition: Recession historically arrives during yield curve steepening, not inversion. Markets celebrate un-inversion as an all-clear signal, but the 1990, 2001, and 2007 recessions all arrived during the steepening phase. Currently relevant with 2s10s at +20-40bp.
evidence: Tier 2b signpost active per prior KB research
5/7 historical easing cycles show recession beginning after steepening commenced
Economic logic is genuine: Fed cutting often means damage already baked in during inversion
generalist_02 provides quantified steepening trade payoff data across 7 cycles
open_questions: Whether current steepening is from expected-rate or term-premium channel
Whether QT era alters the steepening trap dynamics
Does the steepening trap apply to bear-steepening regimes—no empirical test exists?
What specific, falsifiable criteria (2s10s level, decomposition, time horizon) define a steepening trap prediction?
Is the concept unfalsifiable as currently specified given the wide window that can be retroactively fitted?
